yasai silenggi: YASAI SILENGGI tears caused by the wind or by emotion
yasahangge: YASAHANGGE provided with small holes or openings
yasalambi: YASALAMBI to glance at, to look at
yasatabumbi: YASATABUMBI to keep a hawk from closing its eyes during a whole night
yasatu hafan: YASATU HAFAN an ancient designation for censors
yasatu hiyan: YASATU HIYAN a type of incense
yasha: YASHA 1. netting, screen, grating 2. a net woven from the hair of a horse's tail for catching small birds and animals 3. a screen placed on the eaves of the palace to keep birds from nesting there
yasha fa: YASHA FA a window covered with a criss-crossed netlike screen
yashalambi: YASHALAMBI 1. to make netting or screen 2. to make lanterns 3. to place in a net bag
yashalaha dalangga: YASHALAHA DALANGGA a dam with filters
yashangga loho: YASHANGGA LOHO a sword with grill-work designs on the blade
yasuka: YASUKA an eagle resembling the isuka that is found in Liaotung
yatarakū: YATARAKŪ an instrument for striking a fire, a flint
yatarakū fadu: YATARAKŪ FADU a bag for flint
yatarambi: YATARAMBI to strike a fire (with a flint)
yatuhan: YATUHAN a zitherlike instrument having fourteen strings
yaya demun i oci okini: YAYA DEMUN I OCI OKINI let it be as it may
yaya hacin: YAYA HACIN every kind, every sort
yayadambi: YAYADAMBI to lisp, to speak unclearly
ye: YE the secondary beams of a roof, a small beam
yebcungge baita: YEBCUNGGE BAITA affair of the heart
yebecungge: YEBECUNGGE one who has gotten better, convalescent
yebelembi: YEBELEMBI to find amusing, to find pleasure in, to esteem (generally used only in the negative)
yebihen: YEBIHEN a ceiling cover made from paper
yece: YECE a ghost with white hair and bloody wounds
yece hutu: YECE HUTU the same as yece
yecuhe: YECUHE 1. the name of a small black fly 2. flying ant
yedun: YEDUN a deer-head mask worn by hunters who are trying to lure deer
yehe: YEHE 1. a tube on the top of a helmet used for attaching a tassel 2. the surface of an arrowhead 3. white hemp that has been treated in lime water
yeken akū: YEKEN AKŪ lowly, debased, not upright
yelmen: YELMEN one name for the sparrowhawk; cf. silmen
yelu: YELU a boar, a male pig
yelu baimbi: YELU BAIMBI to seek a boar (of a sow)
yemji: YEMJI 1. apparition, ghost 2. a deity of lakes and rivers--it takes the form of a three-year-old child with long ears, red eyes, and beautiful hair
yemjiri gasha: YEMJIRI GASHA one name for the owl
yemjitu dobi: YEMJITU DOBI the name of a mythical reptile on the Yangtze
yen: YEN 1. a mountain path, an animal trail 2. yin--the female principle; cf. e
yendebun: YENDEBUN the name of one of the parts of the Classic of Poetry
yendembi: YENDEMBI to rise, to flourish, to be prosperous
yendengge mudan: YENDENGGE MUDAN the rising tone of classical Chinese phonology
yengke menggun: YENGKE MENGGUN small silver bars of about five ounces weight
yengsi: YENGSI a banquet, a bridal banquet
yenmanggi: YENMANGGI soot on the bottom of a pot
yentu: YENTU 1. a narrow woven belt 2. new feathers that grow next to the large wing feathers on a bird 3. an iron for ironing clothes
yentu cecike: YENTU CECIKE the name of a small bird with brown eyes, blackish beak, white stripes over the eyes, speckled feathers, and yellow legs
yertembi: YERTEMBI to be ashamed, to be embarrassed
yertešembi: YERTEŠEMBI to be mortified from shame
yeru: YERU hole, pit, den (of tigers, panthers, leopards, wildcats, etc.)
yerutu: YERUTU a stone house used by various aboriginal peoples of South China
yeye: YEYE 1. maggot 2. glutinous, sticky 3. sticky mud 4. annoying, obtrusive 5. (paternal) grandfather
yeye handu: YEYE HANDU the same as yeye bete
yeye ira: YEYE IRA glutinous millet YEYE SCEScHUN annoying and loathsome
yeyedembi: YEYEDEMBI to speak in a long-winded, annoying manner
yo: YO 1. a handful; cf. sefere 2. see yoo
yobo: YOBO 1. fun, play, joking 2. a person who likes to play, a merrymaker, mischievous person
yobo maktambi: YOBO MAKTAMBI to make someone laugh by amusing talk, to joke
yobo niyalma: YOBO NIYALMA a mischievous person, a practical joker
yobodombi: YOBODOMBI to have fun, to joke, to make sport of
yocambi: YOCAMBI to itch, to be bitten by bugs
yoge sindambi: YOGE SINDAMBI to perform a service for the dead, to perform the ullambana service during which a miniature pagoda made of fruit is scattered by monks
yoge sindara isan: YOGE SINDARA ISAN a gathering for the ullambana service
yoge sindara karan: YOGE SINDARA KARAN a miniature pagoda made from cakes (a type of offering)
yoge sindara mandal: YOGE SINDARA MANDAL the place where the ullambana service was performed, a service for the dead
yohan suri: YOHAN SURI a type of thin cotton material
yohi: YOHI 1. complete, intact, without gaps 2. a (complete) set
yohibun: YOHIBUN a collection of documents or writings
yohimbi: YOHIMBI (-ka, -ra/ndara) 1. to form a scab 2. to pay heed to, to mind, to pay attention to
yohon: YOHON water ditch in a field
yohoron: YOHORON 1. a waterway in the mountains 2. small stream or canal
yohoron gocimbi: YOHORON GOCIMBI to expand like a dammed stream (said of fattening horses)
yohoronombi: YOHORONOMBI to form a groove or furrow
yojin: YOJIN one name for the peacock; cf. tojin
yojohošombi: YOJOHOŠOMBI to itch to the point where one can't bear it any longer
yokcin akū: YOKCIN AKŪ small and pale, petty, unimpressive
yokcingga: YOKCINGGA having a good appearance, impressive
yokto: YOKTO 1. proper, suitable, meet 2. meaningful 3. see also ai yokto
yokto akū: YOKTO AKŪ 1. improper, unsuitable, embarrassing 2. meaningless, depressed, without enthusiasm
yolo indahūn: YOLO INDAHŪN the name of a dog with a thick head and tail, hanging lips, and big ears
yolo jahūdai: YOLO JAHŪDAI the name of a boat propelled by a scull at the stern
yolo yokto akū: YOLO YOKTO AKŪ thoroughly improper, thoroughly embarrassing
yolonggi: YOLONGGI sparks or cinders that fly out from a fire
